jobs.name: remind-bob-support-inboxes
jobs.prompt: Send Bob a reminder that I still need the support inbox details for FlowInCash and Crispi if he hasn't already sent them. Keep it short, plain English, and include that I’m ready to handle email support once I have them.

jobs.name: auto-update-pi-hermes
jobs.prompt:
Check for updates to Pi and Hermes, using ONLY canonical upstream sources. Two halves: MAINTENANCE (drift detection + auto-bump dev tier / alert-only prod tier) and OPPORTUNITY (what's new in upstream, what could it unlock for Bob's apps).

================================================================
MAINTENANCE
================================================================

PI (Surface 2 — global Pi binary, dev tier — AUTO-BUMP):
  1. Verify the canonical package: run "npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent version" to get latest.
  2. Get current installed version: run "pi --version" (the binary should be the @earendil-works fork; verify by running "ls -la $(which pi)" and confirming the symlink target includes "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent" in the path).
  3. If the symlink path STILL includes "@mariozechner": ABORT updates and Telegram an alert that the migration off the deprecated fork (per dev-team-work-loop/DEV-TEAM-MIGRATION-2026-05-10.md) was not completed. Do NOT auto-install anything to "correct" this — it requires deliberate cleanup.
  4. If on @earendil-works AND outdated: run "npm install -g @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@latest". Verify install succeeded by re-running "pi --version".
  5. NEVER install @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent. NEVER install any package whose npm "deprecated" field is set (check with "npm view <package> deprecated").

HERMES (Surface 1 — local Hermes, dev tier — AUTO-BUMP):
  1. cd /local-AI-Stack/home-hermes/hermes-agent (the canonical NousResearch upstream — verify with "git remote -v" that origin points to github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git).
  2. If origin is NOT NousResearch/hermes-agent: ABORT and Telegram an alert.
  3. Run "git fetch" and check how many commits behind origin/main we are.
  4. If behind: run "hermes update" (or the canonical update mechanism).
  5. Verify version bumped via "hermes --version".

SIDECAR_PI_DEP (Surface 3 — production Sidecar npm dep — ALERT-ONLY, NEVER MODIFY):
  1. cd /media/bob/C/AI_Projects/LivingApp-Sidecar.
     If the directory doesn't exist: ABORT this section, Telegram "Sidecar repo not found at expected path".
  2. Read package.json. Locate dependencies."@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent".
     If missing: ABORT this section, Telegram "Sidecar pi-coding-agent dep missing — investigate".
  3. Parse the version. Post-D9 it is exact-pinned (e.g., "0.74.0"); tolerate caret form by stripping the leading "^".
  4. Query: npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent version  → latest.
  5. Query: npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent time --json  → ISO timestamp of when the pinned version was published.
  6. Compute: days_since_pin = (now - pin_publish_date).days.
  7. DUAL-SOURCE CVE check on the pinned version:
     (a) GitHub Security Advisories: gh api graphql -f query='{ securityVulnerabilities(ecosystem: NPM, package: "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent", first: 10) { nodes { advisory { ghsaId severity summary } vulnerableVersionRange } } }'
     (b) npm audit: from inside /media/bob/C/AI_Projects/LivingApp-Sidecar run "npm audit --json --audit-level=high" and check whether @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent appears in the vulnerabilities map.
     If EITHER source reports a CVE/advisory affecting the pinned version: cve_present = true. Capture which source caught it (helps tune the dual-sourcing decision over time).
  8. Check for npm "deprecated" field: run "npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@<pinned> deprecated".
  9. DO NOT EDIT package.json. Even if outdated, even if CVE present, even if deprecated.
  10. Capture for report: { current_pin, latest, days_since_pin, cve_present, cve_source, deprecated, gate_status }
      where gate_status is one of:
        "PIN_OK"           — within 90 days, no CVE, not deprecated. No action needed.
        "STALE_BUMP_DUE"   — >= 90 days, no CVE. Bump per ADR 002 schedule.
        "CVE_FAST_TRACK"   — CVE filed against pinned version. Bump immediately per NFR16.
        "DEPRECATED_PIN"   — npm deprecated field set on pinned version. Investigate.

HERMES_CONTAINER_TAG (Surface 4 — production Hermes container — ALERT-ONLY, NEVER MODIFY):
  1. cd /media/bob/C/AI_Projects/LivingApp-Hermes.
     If the directory doesn't exist: ABORT this section, Telegram "LivingApp-Hermes repo not found".
  2. Read Dockerfile. grep for "ARG HERMES_TAG=".
     If missing: ABORT this section, Telegram "HERMES_TAG missing from Dockerfile — investigate".
  3. Extract pinned tag (e.g., "v2026.5.7").
  4. Query GitHub: gh api repos/NousResearch/hermes-agent/releases/latest  → latest tag + publish date.
     If `gh` is unavailable: fall back to "git ls-remote --tags https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git | sort -V | tail".
  5. Query GitHub publish date of the PINNED tag: gh api repos/NousResearch/hermes-agent/releases/tags/<TAG>.
  6. Compute days_since_pin.
  7. Query the canonical CVE source for git-tag-pinned upstream Hermes: gh api repos/NousResearch/hermes-agent/security-advisories. (NOT the GraphQL securityVulnerabilities query — that's NPM-only and won't return Hermes results. NOT gh api /repos/.../vulnerability-alerts or /repos/.../dependabot/alerts — those need admin scope on the upstream repo, which we don't have.) Filter the returned advisories to those whose `vulnerabilities[].patched_versions` indicate the pinned tag is affected. If the endpoint returns 404 or an empty list, treat as "no CVE" (not as an abort) — NousResearch may simply not have filed any.
  8. DO NOT EDIT Dockerfile. Even if outdated.
  9. Capture for report: { current_pin, latest_tag, days_since_pin, cve_present, gate_status } per the same enum as above (DEPRECATED_PIN doesn't apply — git tags can't be deprecated; map to PIN_OK if neither stale nor CVE).

================================================================
OPPORTUNITY
================================================================

Track upstream feature velocity so Bob can adopt new capabilities as a market differentiator. For BOTH upstreams, look at releases published in the last 7 days and surface what's new + what it could unlock for Bob's apps (Crispi, FlowInCash, plus 2 future apps in pipeline — all depend on the LivingApp-Sidecar).

PI releases:
  1. Run: npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent versions --json  → all versions.
  2. Run: npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent time --json  → publish timestamps.
  3. Filter to versions published in the last 7 days.
  4. For each, try to fetch release notes: gh api repos/earendil-works/pi/releases (filter by tag matching the version). If no GitHub release exists, fall back to the npm registry README diff or just the version number with "no changelog available".

HERMES releases:
  1. Run: gh api repos/NousResearch/hermes-agent/releases?per_page=30  → recent releases.
  2. Filter to releases published in the last 7 days.
  3. For each: capture title + body (truncate body to ~500 chars).

For each release found, write 1-2 sentence summary in plain English (not a verbatim changelog dump). End each summary with the unlock-assessment in this exact form:
  "Unlocks: <yes — short opportunity statement / no / needs-deeper-look>"

The "yes" path should name the app(s) the new feature could benefit and what it would enable in 1 sentence. Examples:
  "Unlocks: yes — Pi's new streaming output mode could give Crispi real-time progress UI for long auto-fixes."
  "Unlocks: yes — Hermes's new tool-calling shape could simplify FlowInCash's reconciliation skill."
  "Unlocks: needs-deeper-look — new sandboxing flag changes Pi's exec model; worth Friday gold-panning."
  "Unlocks: no — internal refactor only."

If NO releases in the past 7 days for either project: that section reads "No new upstream releases this week."

================================================================
REPORT (single Telegram message — two clearly-labeled sections)
================================================================

🛠 MAINTENANCE
  Pi (global, dev):           <current> → <latest>, action: <none/updated/aborted-with-reason>
  Hermes (local, dev):        <current> → <commits behind>, action: <none/updated/aborted-with-reason>
  Sidecar Pi dep (PROD):      <pinned> (latest <X>, <N> days old, CVE: <y/n>, deprecated: <y/n>) → <gate_status>
  Hermes container (PROD):    <pinned> (latest <X>, <N> days old, CVE: <y/n>) → <gate_status>

  Prefix logic — IMPORTANT: compute the prefix LAST, AFTER every section above has finished and you know the actual gate_status of each surface and whether any ABORT fired. Do NOT emit a prefix optimistically and then retract it. Decide once based on the final state, then prepend ONLY the matching prefix to your delivered message.
    Precedence (highest first; stop at the first match):
    - If any ABORT fired in any section: prefix the WHOLE message with "🚨 MANUAL INTERVENTION NEEDED".
    - Else if any CVE_FAST_TRACK fires: prefix with "⚠ CVE — ACT NOW".
    - Else if any STALE_BUMP_DUE / DEPRECATED_PIN fires: prefix with "⚠ ACTION DUE — see PROD lines below".
    - Else (all PIN_OK, no aborts): NO prefix at all. The clean digest itself is the signal that all is well.

  For each STALE_BUMP_DUE / CVE_FAST_TRACK / DEPRECATED_PIN, append a one-line TODO under the offending PROD line:
    "→ TODO for next dev-team session: open a bump PR on <repo> per ADR 002. Status will carry forward in SESSION-HANDOFF until resolved."

🚀 OPPORTUNITY (last 7 days)
  Pi:
    - <version> (<date>): <1-2 sentence summary>. Unlocks: <…>
    - ... (one per release, max 5; if more, say "+N more — see GitHub")
    [or "No new releases this week"]
  Hermes:
    - <tag> (<date>): <1-2 sentence summary>. Unlocks: <…>
    - ... (one per release, max 5)
    [or "No new releases this week"]

  If any "Unlocks: yes" or "Unlocks: needs-deeper-look" appears: append to message:
    "📌 Friday gold-panning will triage these into ship/park/research-deeper."

  Include a tail of any error output from any section.

NOTES FOR THE EXECUTING AGENT
  - Surfaces 1+2 (dev tier) auto-bump per ADR 002 dev-tier-exception addendum (LivingApp-Sidecar/docs/adr/002-upstream-keep-current-strategy.md → "Addendum: Dev-tier exception"). Don't second-guess this.
  - Surfaces 3+4 (prod tier) NEVER mutate. Even if it would "fix" something. The cron is observation-only on production. Bob (or the next dev-team session) acts on the alert; ADR 002 cost-regression gate must run before any prod bump.
  - When Pulse (Story 3.9) ships, this cron retires (manual disable after Pulse runs clean for 1 month — Bob will flip enabled:false in cron/jobs.json).

jobs.name: friday-gold-panning
jobs.prompt:
Friday gold-panning session. Goal: convert this week's upstream-release opportunities into actionable next-steps for Bob's apps (Crispi, FlowInCash, plus 2 future apps in pipeline; all depend on LivingApp-Sidecar).

This is the FOLLOW-UP to the OPPORTUNITY section of cron 770cfee9f064 (auto-update-pi-hermes), which runs Sunday 03:00 and produces a Telegram digest with "Unlocks: <yes|no|needs-deeper-look>" tags on each release. Five days later (Friday) you triage the week's opportunities into ship/park/research-deeper.

================================================================
STEP 1 — Re-pull the week's upstream release data
================================================================

Independent of any prior digest. Pull releases from BOTH upstreams covering the LAST 7 DAYS:

PI:
  1. Run: npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent versions --json
  2. Run: npm view @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent time --json
  3. Filter to versions published in the last 7 days.
  4. For each version, try gh api repos/earendil-works/pi/releases (filter by tag matching the version) for changelog/notes. Fall back to "no changelog available" if missing.

HERMES:
  1. Run: gh api repos/NousResearch/hermes-agent/releases?per_page=30
  2. Filter to releases published in the last 7 days.
  3. Capture title + body for each (truncate body to ~500 chars).

If NO new releases in the past 7 days for either upstream:
  Telegram a single short line: "Gold-panning <YYYY-MM-DD>: no new opportunities this week." Write nothing else. Exit.

================================================================
STEP 2 — Assess each release through Bob's lens
================================================================

For each release, write a 1-2 sentence summary in plain English (not a verbatim changelog). Then assess what it could unlock for Bob's apps:

Apps to consider:
  - Crispi (live)
  - FlowInCash (live)
  - 2 future apps in pipeline (per session-handoff context — check hermes-dev-team/dev-team-work-loop/SESSION-HANDOFF-*.md for current pipeline state)

For EACH release, classify into one of three buckets:

  SHIP — clear opportunity, low effort, names the app + the feature it unlocks.
    Output: 3-5 line story spec stub:
      Problem: <what user/business problem does this solve?>
      Proposed approach: <how the new upstream feature gets used>
      App target: <Crispi | FlowInCash | future-app-name>
      Estimated effort: <small | medium | large>
      ADR 002 gate impact: <none | needs cost-regression check | needs Pact contract update>

  RESEARCH — promising but needs deeper investigation before commitment.
    Output: 1-paragraph research question + suggested next-session scope (which app, what to investigate, what would unblock a SHIP decision).

  PARK — interesting but not yet useful.
    Output: 1-line entry "<release> — parked: <reason>". Goes into the parked-opportunities log so it can resurface if upstream landscape shifts.

Bob's framing — apply rigorously:
  "Spine of the business — 4 apps depending on it. Stable, done right, not in a rush."
  Default to PARK or RESEARCH unless the SHIP case is clear AND the change is incremental (not a major refactor).
  If you're tempted to put something in SHIP that requires touching ADR 005 architecture or the watchdog composition root, downgrade to RESEARCH instead.

================================================================
STEP 3 — Write the triage handoff file
================================================================

Write the full triage to:
  /media/bob/C/AI_Projects/hermes-dev-team/dev-team-work-loop/GOLD-PANNING-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md

Structure:
  # Gold-Panning Triage — <YYYY-MM-DD>
  ## Sources scanned
    - Pi: <N versions in last 7 days>
    - Hermes: <N releases in last 7 days>
  ## SHIP (<count>)
    [story stubs, one per item]
  ## RESEARCH (<count>)
    [research questions, one per item]
  ## PARK (<count>)
    [parked-opportunities log lines]
  ## Notes
    [anything else worth flagging — upstream pivot signals, unusual release cadence, etc.]

After writing, commit the file to the dev branch on hermes-dev-team:
  git -C /media/bob/C/AI_Projects/hermes-dev-team add dev-team-work-loop/GOLD-PANNING-<DATE>.md
  git -C /media/bob/C/AI_Projects/hermes-dev-team commit -m "docs(gold-panning): <DATE> triage — N SHIP / N RESEARCH / N PARK"
  Do NOT push. Bob pushes manually (push to default branch is auto-mode-blocked).

================================================================
STEP 4 — Telegram Bob a concise summary
================================================================

Single Telegram message, exactly this shape:

🪙 Gold-panning <YYYY-MM-DD>
  SHIP (<count>): <comma-separated short titles, max 80 chars total>
  RESEARCH (<count>): <comma-separated short titles, max 80 chars total>
  PARK (<count>): <comma-separated short titles, max 80 chars total>

  Full triage: dev-team-work-loop/GOLD-PANNING-<DATE>.md (committed to dev branch, not pushed)

  Next: open this with the BMAD story-creation skill to convert SHIP items into stories.

If only PARK items: short message "🪙 Gold-panning <DATE>: <count> items parked, nothing actionable this week."

================================================================
CONSTRAINTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
================================================================

- NEVER edit production code in LivingApp-Sidecar or LivingApp-Hermes. This session is RESEARCH and TRIAGE only.
- NEVER auto-bump pins. Surfaces 3+4 are alert-only per ADR 002 (see LivingApp-Sidecar/docs/adr/002-upstream-keep-current-strategy.md, including the dev-tier addendum).
- Bob is non-technical. Frame all triage outputs in business/feature terms (what does it enable for users / which app benefits / what's the customer-visible change), NOT code/implementation terms.
- Telegram message must be under ~400 chars total — Bob reads on phone.
- Story stubs in the handoff file are SEEDS for next BMAD session, not finished specs. Don't over-design.

If anything blocks execution (gh not available, npm registry down, repo path missing), Telegram a single line "🪙 Gold-panning <DATE>: BLOCKED — <reason>" and exit.
